nativeRange.habitat: Coralline sand areas of clear lagoon and seaward reefs, at depths of 1–25 m

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on small invertebrates and benthic organisms consistent with a trophic level of 3.4. In captivity tends to accept meaty frozen foods such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and chopped seafood; smaller prey items are generally preferred.

avoidWith: dottybacks that may harass smaller gobies

summary: A reef-associated benthic goby from the Indo-Pacific, the Decorated Goby inhabits sandy areas of clear lagoons and seaward reefs. It generally does well in reef aquariums with a sandy substrate and peaceful tankmates.
careNotes:
The Decorated Goby (Istigobius decoratus) is a mid-sized benthic goby reaching around 13 cm, found across the Indo-West Pacific from the Persian Gulf and Red Sea east to Samoa, north to Taiwan, and south to Lord Howe Island. In the wild it tends to be found singly on coralline sand of clear lagoon and seaward reefs at depths of 1–25 m. A sandy substrate in the aquarium is strongly recommended as this species commonly rests on and sifts through sand. It generally adapts reasonably well to captive conditions and tends to accept frozen meaty foods. Standard reef water conditions (temperature 23–29°C, SG 1.020–1.026, pH 8.1–8.4) are generally adequate. Gobies as a group tend to be jumpers; a well-fitted aquarium lid is advisable. This species tends to be kept singly; conspecific aggression in confined spaces has been noted with other benthic gobies sharing the same territory. Note: dedicated aquarium hobby sources with specific husbandry data for this species were unavailable during research; all high-stakes parameters were sourced from FishBase only and should be verified by a reviewer. LiveAquaria (primary source) did not carry this species; Reef2Reef wiki (403), Fishkeeping World (404), Aquatic Community (404), and TFH Magazine (404) also did not have a species-specific page.

RESOLVED 2026-06-03: The flagged concern (LiveAquaria not carrying this species; FishBase as sole source) is resolved. LiveAquaria does carry this species at https://www.liveaquaria.com/products/decorated-goby and provides a full species-specific care page. Quality Marine also carries it with an independent care sheet. Parameters refined as follows: careLevel updated from 'intermediate' to 'easy' (LiveAquaria: Easy; QM: moderate-to-easy); feedingDifficulty updated from 'moderate' to 'easy' (LiveAquaria: straightforward acceptance of standard meaty frozen foods); salinity upper bound corrected from 1.026 to 1.025 (LiveAquaria and QM both list 1.020–1.025); kH populated with 8–12 dKH (confirmed by LiveAquaria and QM). Temperature range kept at FishBase 23–29°C rather than LiveAquaria's 64–77°F (17.8–25°C) — the FishBase range is ecologically derived from field data and more appropriate for a tropical reef species; LiveAquaria's broad lower bound likely reflects tolerance limits rather than recommended range. FishBase moved to additional sources; LiveAquaria reinstated as primary. Quality Marine added as additional source. One minor discrepancy noted: Quality Marine flagged possible coral nipping; all other sources (LiveAquaria, FishBase, majority of hobby literature) rate this species reef safe. Reef safe remains 'yes'.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species-specific page confirmed. Provided care level (Easy), minimum tank size (20 gal / ~75 L), temperature (64–77°F / 17.8–25°C), specific gravity (1.020–1.025), pH (8.1–8.4), dKH (8–12), diet (carnivore: live/frozen brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, black worms, carnivore preparations), reef safe (Yes), temperament (Peaceful; territorial with conspecifics). Refugium strongly recommended as continuous live food source.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed scientific name, taxonomy, adult size (13.0 cm TL), native range (Indo-West Pacific: Persian Gulf/Red Sea to Samoa, north to Taiwan, south to Lord Howe Island), habitat (coralline sand, clear lagoon and seaward reefs, 1–25 m depth), temperature range (23–29°C — more conservative than LiveAquaria's 17.8–25°C; FishBase range used for waterParameters as it is ecologically derived and reef-appropriate), diet (carnivorous, trophic level 3.4), IUCN Least Concern.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Wholesale trade source. Confirmed minimum tank ~30 gal, temperature 72–78°F, salinity 1.020–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12, diet (frozen/live meaty foods), difficulty moderate-to-easy. Noted possible coral nipping behavior (minority finding; LiveAquaria and majority sources list reef safe Yes). May require live foods initially during acclimation.
